event: 20-day history of right upper limb edema
physical examination revealed 3.5 cm mass on right chest
computed tomography (CT) revealed 4 cm mass near right lung hilum
no obvious tumor found in abdominal CT
no perforation found in abdominal CT
biopsy of right thoracic wall nodule metastases showed low differentiated lung adenocarcinoma
abdominal CT scan revealed free air in peritoneal cavity
abdominal CT scan revealed massive ascites in peritoneal cavity
large amount of dirty ascites found during surgery
pus adhesion in small intestine surface
2 perforations (1x1 cm2) at 110 cm and 140 cm of jejunum from Treitz's ligament
2 suspicious perforations of serous membrane at 90 cm and 160 cm of jejunum
pathological diagnosis of resected specimen indicated metastatic lung adenocarcinoma of small intestine
death occurred 19 days following cancer diagnosis
